title: hieracium murorum subsp. subchloroticum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. subchloroticum, also known as wall hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium murorum plant, which belongs to the dandelion family and is native to Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of wall hawkweed include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and promoting digestion, and it has been traditionally used to treat various ailments. Wall hawkweed is sometimes used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to have therapeutic effects, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
